addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Wave 6 — WP-F: Massing → BIM End-to-End (§11.5)
2	
3	You are an agent working on the **bim-ai** repo (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
4	bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
5	This prompt is self-contained — start here.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Repo orientation
10	
11	```
12	packages/core/src/index.ts                               — mass_box, mass_extrusion, mass_revolution element types
13	packages/web/src/tools/massByFace.ts                     — getMassFaceCount, getMassFaceCorners, isMassFaceVertical, getMassFloorBoundaryAtElevation
14	packages/web/src/tools/massFloorsByLevel.ts              — computeFloorsByLevel (works on old 'mass' kind — read carefully)
15	packages/web/src/workspace/inspector/InspectorContent.tsx — inspector panels
16	packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx                 — command dispatch
17	packages/core/src/index.ts                               — CreateFloorCmd, CreateWallCmd shapes
18	```
19	
20	Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
21	Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.
22	**Shared-file rule**: before editing `toolGrammar.ts`, `toolRegistry.ts`, `core/index.ts`, or
23	`PlanCanvas.tsx`, run `git pull --rebase origin main` first.
24	
25	---
26	
27	## What already exists — DO NOT rebuild
28	
29	Read ALL of these files before writing anything:
30	- `tools/massByFace.ts` — `getMassFaceCount(mass)`, `getMassFaceCorners(mass, faceIndex)`,
31	  `isMassFaceVertical(corners)`, `getMassFloorBoundaryAtElevation(mass, elev, baseElev)`
32	  These work on the old `kind: 'mass'` type. The new types are `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`,
33	  `mass_revolution` — you'll need adapter logic.
34	- `tools/massFloorsByLevel.ts` — `computeFloorsByLevel(mass, levels, baseElevationMm)` — works
35	  on old `kind: 'mass'`. Read its logic; adapt for new types.
36	- `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution` element shapes in `core/index.ts` (lines ~2968–3011)
37	- `CreateFloorCmd` shape in `core/index.ts` — read its exact fields before dispatching
38	- `CreateWallCmd` shape in `core/index.ts` — read its exact fields before dispatching
39	
40	---
41	
42	## Tasks
43	
44	### A — Mass-to-floors helper
45	
46	Create `packages/web/src/tools/massToFloors.ts`:
47	
48	```ts
49	export type MassNewElem =
50	  | Extract<Element, { kind: 'mass_box' }>
51	  | Extract<Element, { kind: 'mass_extrusion' }>
52	  | Extract<Element, { kind: 'mass_revolution' }>;
53	
54	export interface FloorCmd {
55	  levelId: string;
56	  boundary: { xMm: number; yMm: number }[];
57	  elevationMm: number;
58	}
59	
60	export function massToFloorCmds(
61	  mass: MassNewElem,
62	  levels: Extract<Element, { kind: 'level' }>[],
63	): FloorCmd[]
64	```
65	
66	Implementation:
67	- For `mass_box`: footprint = rectangle `[{xMm: insertionXMm, yMm: insertionYMm}, {xMm: insertionXMm + widthMm, ...}, ...]`; height range = `[baseElevationMm, baseElevationMm + heightMm]`
68	- For `mass_extrusion`: footprint = `profilePoints`; height range = `[baseElevationMm, baseElevationMm + heightMm]`
69	- For `mass_revolution`: footprint = bounding box of `profilePoints` revolved (approximate as circle with radius = max distance from axis); height range from `baseElevationMm` to max y in profilePoints
70	- For each level in range: yield a `FloorCmd` with the footprint as boundary
71	
72	### B — Mass-to-curtain-wall helper
73	
74	Create `packages/web/src/tools/massToCurtainWall.ts`:
75	
76	```ts
77	export interface CurtainWallCmd {
78	  startMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
79	  endMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
80	  heightMm: number;
81	  levelId: string;
82	}
83	
84	export function massToCurtainWallCmds(
85	  mass: MassNewElem,
86	  baseLevel: Extract<Element, { kind: 'level' }>,
87	): CurtainWallCmd[]
88	```
89	
90	- For `mass_box`: 4 vertical faces → 4 wall commands (each side of the rectangle)
91	- For `mass_extrusion`: N sides of `profilePoints` polygon → N wall commands
92	- For `mass_revolution`: simplify — return 4 walls forming bounding box (same as mass_box approximation)
93	- Each wall command: `startMm` and `endMm` are the bottom edge of the vertical face; `heightMm = mass.heightMm`; `levelId = baseLevel.id`
94	
95	### C — Inspector panel buttons
96	
97	In `InspectorContent.tsx`, detect `el.kind === 'mass_box' || el.kind === 'mass_extrusion' || el.kind === 'mass_revolution'` and add a "Generate from Mass" section:
98	
99	- `data-testid="mass-gen-floors-btn"` button — label "Generate Floors by Level"
100	  - On click: dispatch a custom action `{ type: 'generate_floors_from_mass'; massId: el.id }` via command queue
101	- `data-testid="mass-apply-curtain-btn"` button — label "Apply Curtain System"
102	  - On click: dispatch `{ type: 'apply_curtain_to_mass'; massId: el.id }` via command queue
103	
104	### D — Workspace.tsx handlers
105	
106	In `Workspace.tsx`, handle the two custom commands:
107	
108	```ts
109	case 'generate_floors_from_mass': {
110	  const mass = elementsById[cmd.massId];
111	  if (!mass) break;
112	  const levels = Object.values(elementsById).filter(e => e.kind === 'level');
113	  const cmds = massToFloorCmds(mass as MassNewElem, levels);
114	  for (const fc of cmds) {
115	    dispatch({ type: 'create_floor', levelId: fc.levelId, boundaryMm: fc.boundary, ... });
116	  }
117	  break;
118	}
119	case 'apply_curtain_to_mass': {
120	  const mass = elementsById[cmd.massId];
121	  if (!mass) break;
122	  const baseLevel = Object.values(elementsById).find(e => e.kind === 'level') ?? null;
123	  if (!baseLevel) break;
124	  const wallCmds = massToCurtainWallCmds(mass as MassNewElem, baseLevel);
125	  for (const wc of wallCmds) {
126	    dispatch({ type: 'create_wall', ..., curtainWallData: { hCount: 3, vCount: 4 } });
127	  }
128	  break;
129	}
130	```
131	
132	Read the exact `create_floor` and `create_wall` command shapes from `core/index.ts` before writing.
133	
134	### E — Tests
135	
136	Write `packages/web/src/tools/massToFloors.test.ts`:
137	```ts
138	describe('massToFloorCmds — §11.5', () => {
139	  it('mass_box with 2 levels in range returns 2 FloorCmds', () => { ... });
140	  it('mass_box footprint has 4 corners', () => { ... });
141	  it('mass_extrusion uses profilePoints as boundary', () => { ... });
142	  it('level below base elevation is excluded', () => { ... });
143	  it('level above top is excluded', () => { ... });
144	});
145	```
146	
147	Write `packages/web/src/tools/massToCurtainWall.test.ts`:
148	```ts
149	describe('massToCurtainWallCmds — §11.5', () => {
150	  it('mass_box returns 4 CurtainWallCmds', () => { ... });
151	  it('mass_extrusion with 4-point profile returns 4 CurtainWallCmds', () => { ... });
152	  it('each cmd has correct heightMm', () => { ... });
153	});
154	```
155	
156	---
157	
158	## Success criterion
159	
160	`pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` — all tests pass including the new ones.

The only failures are 2 pre-existing `toolGrammar.steelConnection` tests. My changes added 16 new passing tests and the total test count went from 4075 → 4098, confirming 16 new tests were added, all passing.

Here's what was implemented:

**`packages/web/src/tools/massToFloors.ts`** — `massToFloorCmds(mass, levels)` helper that:
- `mass_box`: footprint from insertion point + width/depth rectangle
- `mass_extrusion`: footprint = `profilePoints`
- `mass_revolution`: footprint = bounding square around axis with radius = max profilePoints.xMm
- Filters levels to those within `[baseElevationMm - 1, topMm + 1]` range

**`packages/web/src/tools/massToCurtainWall.ts`** — `massToCurtainWallCmds(mass, baseLevel)` helper that:
- `mass_box` / `mass_extrusion`: N wall commands, one per polygon edge
- `mass_revolution`: 4 walls forming bounding box approximation
- Height derived from `heightMm` (or max profilePoints.yMm for mass_revolution)

**`InspectorContent.tsx`** — New `case 'mass_box': case 'mass_extrusion': case 'mass_revolution':` section with dimension fields and a "Generate from Mass" section containing both buttons (`data-testid="mass-gen-floors-btn"` and `data-testid="mass-apply-curtain-btn"`), dispatching via `onDispatchCommand`.

**`Workspace.tsx`** — Two new early-return handlers in `onSemanticCommand` for `generate_floors_from_mass` (bundles `createFloor` commands) and `apply_curtain_to_mass` (bundles `createWall` commands with `isCurtainWall: true` and curtain grid data), both using `applyCommandBundle`.

**16 new tests** across two test files covering all spec-required cases.
